Voltage Drop Calculator

Calculate voltage drop in electrical wiring. Enter wire gauge, length, current, and voltage to determine if your wiring is adequate.

FAQ

What is acceptable voltage drop?

NEC recommends no more than 3% voltage drop for branch circuits and 5% total (feeder + branch). For a 120V circuit, that's 3.6V max on a branch.

How to reduce voltage drop?

Use thicker wire (lower AWG), shorten the wire run, or reduce the current load. Doubling wire size cuts voltage drop in half.
